Class EffectMap<K extends NormalizableEffect>

java.lang.Object
it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.objects.AbstractObject2DoubleFunction<K>
it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.objects.AbstractObject2DoubleMap<K>
net.fokinatorr.sparkle.effect.EffectMap<K>
Type Parameters:
K - the effect type
All Implemented Interfaces:
it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.Function<K,Double>, it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.objects.Object2DoubleFunction<K>, it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.objects.Object2DoubleMap<K>, Serializable, Function<K,Double>, ToDoubleFunction<K>, Map<K,Double>
Direct Known Subclasses:
GraphicEffectMap, SoundEffectMap
public abstract class EffectMap<K extends NormalizableEffect> extends it.unimi.dsi.fastutil.objects.AbstractObject2DoubleMap<K>
An object mapping effects to their values.

Null keys are not supported. The map contains all the possible keys, so removing a key effectively sets the associated value to its default value.

The default return value of an effect map is 0.0 and it can't be changed.

See Also:

  • Field Details

    • values

      protected final AtomicDoubleArray values
      The values array. Similarly to EnumMap, maps enum ordinal to value.

    • kClass

      protected final Class<K extends NormalizableEffect> kClass
      The effect class, stored because of type erasure

    • effects

      protected final K extends NormalizableEffect[] effects
      All the possible effect values.

  • Constructor Details

    • EffectMap

      protected EffectMap(K[] effects)
      Creates a new empty EffectMap with the given possible effect values.
      Parameters:
      effects - the possible effect values
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the effect array size is 0
      NullPointerException - if the effects array is null

      public abstract EffectMap<K> copy()
      Creates and returns a deep copy of this effect map. The copied map's contents are independent of this map's contents.
      Returns:
      a deep copy of this map
